title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: AWS Power Hour: Generative AI For Developers | S1 E1 | Exam Overview and FM Integrations

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This episode explains the exam format and study strategies while guiding developers through designing resilient Generative AI architectures. It covers building data processing pipelines for foundation models, implementing vector stores for RAG systems, prompt engineering techniques, and analyzes sample exam questions with real-world solutions.
bullets: Breaks down exam format and offers study strategies for developers.
Guides design of resilient Generative AI architectures.
Explains building data processing pipelines for foundation models.
Covers implementing vector stores for RAG systems and prompt engineering.
Analyzes sample exam questions and discusses real-world advanced GenAI solutions.
takeaways: Ensure infrastructure plans support resilient GenAI architectures to meet availability and reliability needs.
Provision sufficient compute and storage for foundation-model data processing pipelines.
Design for low-latency, high-throughput storage and retrieval to support vector stores used in RAG.
Prioritize staff training and certification/study paths so teams can build and operate advanced GenAI systems.

items.title: re:Invent - Chalk Talk - Solving private IPv4 exhaustion using AWS Cloud WAN & Amazon VPC Lattice

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this session addresses private IPv4 exhaustion caused by limited address planning, architectural changes, mergers, and IP‑hungry technologies such as containers. It explains a scalable, cost‑effective approach using AWS Cloud WAN with service insertion and Amazon VPC Lattice to centralize private NAT Gateways and AWS PrivateLink, maximizing IP utilization, lowering costs, and simplifying network management across distributed environments.
bullets: Explains causes of private IPv4 exhaustion (planning limits, mergers, containers, architectural shifts).
Presents a combined solution: AWS Cloud WAN with service insertion plus Amazon VPC Lattice.
Centralizes private NAT Gateways and AWS PrivateLink to reduce per‑VPC IP usage.
Aims to maximize IP space utilization and reduce operational and infrastructure costs.
Focuses on simplifying network management and providing a durable, scalable solution.
takeaways: Centralizing NAT and PrivateLink can free up scarce private IPv4 addresses and cut IP consumption per VPC.
Using AWS Cloud WAN with service insertion plus VPC Lattice enables scalable IP use across distributed environments, aiding future growth.
The approach reduces cost and operational complexity, making new project deployment and cross‑environment integration easier.
This architecture provides a durable way to manage IP constraints arising from mergers and shifting infrastructure patterns.

bullets: Evan Bates highlights the promising future of figure skating.

No transcript is available for the video.
Team USA prompts viewers to subscribe and follow across social platforms.
Video is associated with #WinterOlympics.
takeaways: Olympic-related short clips can contribute to spikes in streaming and social traffic—monitor capacity around event timelines.
Ensure CDN and bandwidth readiness for distribution of short-form athlete-focused content.
Use analytics from Team USA channels to predict demand and pre-warm caches for popular clips.
Coordinate with platform teams to auto-scale infrastructure during major sports events and promotional pushes.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description… In 2015 Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York intending to build Airbnb properties. Over the next three years he commuted on weekends from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin himself, added more cabins, and as of September 2025 the property has brought in over $2.1 million.
bullets: 2015 purchase of a five-acre property in upstate New York to develop Airbnb rentals.
Chris Broomfield built the first A-frame cabin himself through weekend commutes from Connecticut over three years.
Additional cabins were added to the property over time.
By September 2025 the property had generated more than $2.1 million in revenue.
Project grew from a single self-built cabin into a multi-cabin short-term rental operation.
takeaways: Small rural land developments can scale into high-revenue short-term rental clusters, which may create concentrated new local electricity demand that warrants monitoring for capacity planning.
Incremental, phased construction and occupancy mean utilities might see staggered increases in load over several years—consider planning for phased infrastructure upgrades.

High-end short-term rentals are likely to prioritize reliable power and amenities, indicating potential need for resilient supply options (e.g., backup power or higher service levels) in areas seeing similar development.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Eksperci KPMG omawiają, jak zmiany operacyjne (np. przeniesienie produkcji, dystrybucji, zespołów) powinny być oceniane z perspektywy cen transferowych i wycen oraz jakie obowiązki raportowe i dokumentacyjne mogą się pojawić. Podkreślają znaczenie wczesnego przygotowania dokumentacji, analiz EBIT i właściwych wycen, aby uniknąć problemów i pułapek przy transferze pracowników czy kontraktów.
bullets: Definicja restrukturyzacji w kontekście cen transferowych i gdy zmiana jest istotna (analiza wpływu na EBIT).
Znaczenie dokumentacji restrukturyzacji przygotowanej „na czas” — zmniejsza ryzyko i stres w przyszłości.
Obowiązki raportowe, w tym TPR i MDR, które mogą wyniknąć z reorganizacji.
Rola wycen i najczęstsze błędy przy ustalaniu wartości i alokacji funkcji/ryzyk.
Przykłady praktyczne: przeniesienie produkcji/dystrybucji, transfer pracowników, przeniesienie kontraktów i checklisty działań.
takeaways: Zanim przeniesiesz infrastrukturę, usługi lub zespoły, oceń wpływ na marże/EBIT z perspektywy cen transferowych.
Przygotuj pełną dokumentację restrukturyzacyjną z wyprzedzeniem — to zmniejsza ryzyko korekt i sporów.
Sprawdź obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) przy każdej istotnej zmianie, by uniknąć sankcji lub dodatkowych wymogów informacyjnych.
Zleć niezależne wyceny i uwzględnij typowe pułapki przy transferze kontraktów i pracowników.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Marcus Oliver of Google Delta says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy—not compute costs. He advises organizations to reimagine value chains and deploy multi-agent systems to achieve real business outcomes.
bullets: Biggest mistake: rushing to code without an overarching AI strategy.
Compute costs are not the primary expense—lack of strategy is.
Focus on reimagining value chains to identify where AI adds real value.
Deploy multi-agent systems as a means to operationalize complex AI workflows.
Engage professional services (Google Delta) to guide strategy and execution.
takeaways: Prioritize strategy and value-chain analysis before scaling or provisioning compute.
Design data center and grid investments to support multi-agent, orchestration-heavy workloads, not just raw compute.
Allocate budget for planning, integration, and professional services as part of AI ROI, not only hardware.
Ensure infrastructure decisions map directly to measurable business outcomes to avoid costly rework.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform designed to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing visibility and protection against evolving threats. It highlights three capabilities—AI Lens for visibility and policy application, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing models and apps, and an AI Firewall for real-time prompt and response guardrails.
bullets: Provides centralized visibility to eliminate shadow AI across user interactions.
AI Lens lets teams apply policies and monitor AI system use.
AI Red Teaming performs real-world attack simulations against models and applications.
AI Firewall enforces real-time guardrails on prompts and responses.
Hosted on Google Cloud as a unified platform to protect AI agents and infrastructure.
takeaways: Visibility across AI usage is essential to detect and eliminate unmanaged or ‘‘shadow’’ AI that can threaten infrastructure.
Regular red-teaming of models and apps helps identify vulnerabilities before they are exploited in production.
Real-time prompt/response controls reduce risk from malicious or unsafe AI outputs entering operational systems.
A unified, cloud-based platform can simplify policy enforcement and reduce the operational burden of securing distributed AI agents.

bullets: Focus areas: AI-first infrastructure, smart mobility, clean energy, digital payments, and vernacular AI.
Goal: enable inclusive, resilient, and future-ready growth across sectors.
Alignment with national missions such as Atmanirbhar Bharat and Gati Shakti.
Emphasis on shared digital futures for citizens, industries, and communities.
Positioning as a strategic partner in India's accelerating growth story.
takeaways: Prioritize AI-ready infrastructure and compute capacity to support AI-first solutions and vernacular AI workloads.
Plan for integration with clean energy and smart mobility initiatives to ensure resilient, sustainable operations.
Consider collaboration opportunities with vendors like Hitachi India that align with national programs (Atmanirbhar Bharat, Gati Shakti).
Design systems for inclusive access and interoperability to serve citizens, industries, and community-focused digital services.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, GeForce Garage announced the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build: u/JeffersonPutnam, who built a small-form-factor system featuring a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition. The video description lists the full build specs (with a PCPartPicker link) and notes GeForce host Dwight (@_MightyPotato).
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 build.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition; Motherboard: MSI MPG B850I Edge.
CPU: Ryzen 7 9800X3D; RAM: 32GB Teamgroup T-Create Expert DDR5-6000.
Power: Corsair SF1000; Cooling: Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO; full parts list on PCPartPicker.
Host and links: GeForce host Dwight (@_MightyPotato) and official GeForce social accounts included.
takeaways: An RTX 5090 was integrated into a small-form-factor chassis — indicates high-end consumer GPUs can be packaged into compact systems.
The build uses a 1000W SFX power supply (Corsair SF1000), highlighting substantial power provisioning needs for top-tier GPUs in compact enclosures.
Mini-ITX platform (MSI MPG B850I Edge) paired with Ryzen 7 9800X3D shows compatibility of powerful CPU/GPU combos on compact motherboards—useful when evaluating dense or compact node designs.
A full PCPartPicker parts list is provided, enabling quick review of component choices, compatibility and sourcing for prototyping similar systems.
